tracked.name: NLP Padding: Body Flooding to Outweigh Malicious Signals
tracked.description: Attackers append large volumes of benign, unrelated text after several line breaks at the end of phishing emails to dilute the malicious signal in NLP-based content classifiers. Watch for: wider adoption of this technique across campaigns, variations (HTML comment stuffing, zero-font text, invisible Unicode), vendor research on detection countermeasures, and any PhaaS kits incorporating it as a standard evasion feature.
